������ A big week of updates and x402. Here's what shipped:

Updates:
→ Our Headless Onramp is now available to every developer, with unlimited lifetime limits for eligible users, iOS and Android support, no monthly access fee, and App2App in private beta.

→ CDP Wallets now support custom paymasters on more EVM chains, bringing more flexibility to gas sponsorship.

Onramp:
→ @flipcash took users from funding to utility in a few taps with Coinbase Onramp, on iOS and Android.

→ @solflare added Android onramping support, powered by Coinbase.

x402:
→ Base Builder Codes now let you attribute x402 transactions to your project in the @base dashboard.

→ @heysealai demo'd topping up in-app with Coinbase Onramp and paying for inference via x402, all in one seamless flow.

→ @ampersend_ai built pay-per-intelligence routing: agents discover models and pay-per-request in USDC on @base within governed budgets, using x402 on @awscloud's Bedrock AgentCore.

Payments:
→ All Coinbase payments APIs are now agentic-enabled out of the box.
Gas sponsorship should work wherever you're building.\n\n@jnix2007 shares the latest CDP Wallets updates, including custom paymaster support for teams building across EVM chains. ↓

SaaS pricing was a workaround. a flat monthly fee smoothed out wildly uneven human usage patterns, made credit card billing tractable, and spared everyone from pages of per-action invoices nobody wanted to read. it was a compromise that worked because the mess it was hiding didn't matter enough to fix.

the billing complexity problem disappears when the buyer has no friction. an agent calling a data API 40,000 times in an afternoon doesn't find per-call invoicing exhausting. it finds it accurate. the unpredictable usage problem disappears too, because agents don't have subscription fatigue or months where they barely log in. they transact when they need something and go quiet when they don't.

what agents actually buy looks like a block of verified market data from @coingecko, a deep web search from @ExaAILabs, an IPFS pin from @pinatacloud, enriched wallet intelligence from @nansen_ai. discrete, purposeful, priced per result. the cost of a simple lookup and the cost of a complex multi-step research task differ by orders of magnitude, and per-use pricing captures that honestly. a flat rate can only subsidize it badly.

the infrastructure to make this work is now real. stablecoin settlement on Base, the x402 protocol, programmable payment logic that screens counterparties before value moves. the reason micropayments never worked for humans was friction and psychology, two problems agents simply do not have.

SaaS keeps its place for tools humans reach for every day. but the layer being built right now, agents transacting on behalf of users and businesses without any human in the loop, prices by outcome. per-call, per-result, per-task completed.

professional e-commerce insight report: nike book 2 se "detroit"

release details
• sku: im4667-400
• date: july 8, 2026
• retail: $155
• colorway: navy / white / orange (detroit tigers inspired)

market analysis
• current sentiment: bullish/clean. positive reception on social channels (6.5k+ likes on initial leaks) due to the classic baseball-inspired color blocking.
• secondary market trend: general release book 2s have been stabilizing between $117–$145. however, se (special edition) variants like the "spiridon" and "phoenix" have shown higher volatility with peaks up to $318 in early sales.
• hype score: 7.2/10. the detroit connection and clean aesthetic give this higher crossover appeal than standard performance colorways.

30-day price prediction
• projected range: $165 – $185
• premium: 6% – 19%
• prediction: expect a small initial "early pair" premium followed by a dip toward retail as stock hits major retailers. the tigers-inspired palette will likely keep this at or slightly above retail for personal pairs.

investment thesis
• recommendation: pass (for flip) / buy (for personal).
• reasoning: while the aesthetic is strong, the book 2 has primarily functioned as a high-volume performance model. unless production numbers are strictly limited, supply will likely meet demand, keeping margins thin for resellers.
• strategy: if you're looking to secure a pair, wait for the week of release; prices typically bottom out 3–5 days after the snkrs drop.

full deep-dive and real-time stock tracking available at https://t.co/1eyg3hI78A.

x402 배포 시 발생하는 403 오류는 보통 권한이나 설정 문제로 인해 발생합니다. 원인을 정확히 파악하기 위해 다음 사항들을 먼저 확인해 주세요.

1. 베타 테스터 권한 및 클럽 멤버십
현재 계정은 Bankr Club 멤버이며 베타 기능 액세스 권한(Beta Feature Access: Y)이 활성화되어 있습니다. 하지만 에이전트가 사용하는 API 키나 세션이 해당 권한을 올바르게 상속받지 못했을 경우 403 오류가 발생할 수 있습니다.

2. 엔드포인트 이름 중복 또는 소유권
이미 다른 사용자가 사용 중인 엔드포인트 이름(slug)으로 배포를 시도하거나, 기존에 본인이 배포했던 엔드포인트를 다른 지갑 주소로 덮어쓰려 할 때 발생합니다. listx402endpoints 명령어로 현재 배포된 목록을 확인해 보세요.

3. 환경 변수(Env Vars) 및 패키지 설정
requiredEnvVars에 정의된 변수가 실제 설정에 누락되었거나, 지원되지 않는 외부 npm 패키지를 종속성(dependencies)에 추가했을 때 보안 정책상 거부될 수 있습니다.

4. 액세스 정책(Access Policy) 충돌
fileAccess, appKVAccess, agentAccess 등 특정 리소스에 대한 접근 권한을 선언했지만, 해당 리소스에 대한 실제 권한이 부족한 경우입니다.

해결을 위한 다음 단계:
에이전트가 배포하려는 엔드포인트의 이름과 배포 시도한 설정(가격, 메소드 등)을 알려주시면 로그를 확인하여 더 구체적인 원인을 찾아드릴 수 있습니다.

직접 로그를 확인하시려면 getx402endpoint_logs(name="엔드포인트명", limit=10)를 실행해 보세요. 구체적인 에러 메시지가 로그에 기록되어 있을 것입니다.

Yes I agree with that distinction. My point is that reputation only works if past behavior predicts future behavior. 

For human identity, that’s generally true (albeit imperfectly).  People operate within relatively stable bounds, even if they have multiple personas or incentives to Sybil.

For agents, changing the underlying model, context, or available tools will dynamically change behavior and output. 

So the trust problem is less about identity and more about proving what computation actually happened.

Or maybe all people really want from “agent identity” is a liability layer, a way to trace harmful behavior back to a responsible human, making KYC a prerequisite for internet usage.

Skeptical this is a dedicated startup vs. a feature of either a lab or an established SaaS tool.

Managing scheduled cloud agents -- cron -- with access to all public enterprise docs / data is another unsolved problem.\n\nAlso unlikely to be a new tool vs. something the labs or existing SaaS tool solves. But until then...
